requirements.statement: Treat workflow YAML and repository settings as the delivery implementation; accept green evidence only for the exact commit, inputs, toolchain, and workflow revision.
Run the same canonical verification commands for pull requests, merge groups, and `main`.
Do not maintain a weaker duplicate implementation of a check.
Pin toolchains, dependencies, action revisions, and container bases.
Keep pins on the newest stable, mutually compatible releases through automated pull requests.
Run untrusted PR code without repository, package, cloud, or deployment credentials and with read-only token authority except in an isolated trusted job.
Build deployable artifacts only from a verified `main` or release commit.
Identify deployable artifacts by immutable digest.
Promote deployable artifacts by rebuilding them.
Do not let pre-commit hooks replace server-side required checks or let hook bypass bypass CI.
Retain test reports, relevant logs, browser traces, coverage, SBOMs, attestations, and scan results long enough to diagnose the failing commit.
Do not claim production deployment before a hosted target exists.
Keep `main` as the default branch.
Protect `main` with a ruleset or equivalent branch protection.
Forbid direct pushes to `main` except through an explicitly audited break-glass role.
Require a pull request before changes enter `main`.
Require the current Section 5 checks.
Require all review conversations to be resolved.
Require a current head branch or merge-queue validation against the protected base.
Require one approval once the repository has more than one maintainer.
Dismiss or invalidate approval when the reviewed diff changes materially.
Validate the exact merge-group commit when merge queue is enabled.
Use squash merging so `main` receives one intentional commit per PR.
Enforce the repository Conventional Commit subset for PR titles.
Disable merge commits.
Disable force pushes to `main`.
Once an independent owner exists, use `CODEOWNERS` to protect workflows, dependency/toolchain config, containers, migrations, auth, sandbox/process control, and release config.
Preserve CI and conversation-resolution requirements in the sole-maintainer exception.
Keep required-check names stable.
Update the ruleset in the same administrative change as a workflow refactor.
Do not leave `main` unguarded during workflow/ruleset changes.
Separate reusable verification from trigger-specific CI, CodeQL, publication, and release authority.
Trigger `ci.yml` on pull requests, merge groups, and pushes to `main`.
Enable the merge-group trigger when its check is required by a merge-queue ruleset.
Do not substitute a manual diagnostic run for a required check on another SHA.
Cancel an older run for the same pull request.
Do not cancel `main`, merge-group, release, or deployment runs merely because a newer commit appears.
Make workflows call repository-owned Make targets.
Use YAML only to coordinate jobs and permissions, not duplicate build logic.
Make the same `make verify` composition runnable on a contributor host.
Give every workflow top-level `contents: read` or empty permissions and grant extra token rights only to the job that needs them.
Do not give build or test jobs write permissions.
Pin all executed GitHub and third-party actions to full commit SHAs.
Put the corresponding release tag in a nearby action-pin comment.
Do not execute floating action tags such as `@main`, `@latest`, or an unpinned major.
Keep action SHAs current through automated dependency updates.
Do not use `pull_request_target` to check out or execute untrusted PR content.
A future metadata-only `pull_request_target` workflow must not fetch, evaluate, import, or interpolate executable PR data.
Require an explicit threat-model review for any future metadata-only `pull_request_target` workflow.
Do not interpolate untrusted titles, refs, messages, bodies, or inputs directly into shell scripts.
Pass untrusted values through environment variables or structured inputs and quote them in the receiving program.
Do not expose secrets to ordinary PR jobs.
Give fork PRs the same validation that can run safely without secrets.
Use protected GitHub Environments for deployment jobs.
Use OIDC where the target supports it instead of long-lived cloud credentials.
Enable GitHub secret scanning and push protection when the plan permits.
Run a repository secret scanner without giving it secrets.
Make workflow changes pass actionlint before merge.
Make workflow changes pass zizmor before merge.
Review generated workflow changes as rigorously as handwritten YAML.
Use Gitleaks for the repository secret scan.
Scan the final container filesystem with Trivy when a Dockerfile exists.
Keep actionlint, zizmor, Gitleaks, and Trivy as build-time controls, not runtime application dependencies, under the Section 8 pin policy.
Run CodeQL or equivalent TypeScript SAST on PRs, pushes to `main`, and a regular schedule.
Upload SAST results to GitHub code scanning and identify the analyzed commit.
Block newly introduced actionable findings under a configured severity policy.
Require reviewed reason and scope for a SAST suppression.
Expose stable required checks in the PR graph.
Fail final `ci / required` unless every required job passed or returned an explicitly permitted result.
Reject conflict markers, whitespace errors, and unexpected generated drift.
Validate Markdown links and normative formatting with Markdownlint CLI2.
Lint GitHub Actions and container definitions when present.
Validate the pnpm lockfile and workspace graph.
Check the PR title convention.
Keep a required workflow terminal when path filtering skips work inside a job.
Do not let GitHub path filters omit a required check.
Run manifest-aware dependency review when a supported manifest or lockfile changes.
Reject newly introduced packages above the vulnerability threshold or with forbidden licenses.
Surface passing dependency additions for maintenance, documentation, size, native-build, and replacement-cost review.
Gate workflow-action changes with full-SHA validation, actionlint, and zizmor.
Do not cite package dependency review as evidence about executed action code.
Resolve container-base changes to an immutable digest.
Use generated image SBOM, vulnerability scan, and configured license policy as final-image evidence.
Install with the frozen lockfile for static verification.
Run Biome check in static verification.
Run no-emit TypeScript checking.
Run package-boundary and cycle checks.
Run generated OpenAPI and client drift checks.
Fail CI rather than rewrite files.
Run Vitest unit, contract, and component suites with deterministic time and randomness.
Do not retry a failed test into green without preserving the first failure and marking the test flaky.
Collect coverage from the complete unit/contract suite.
Base the initial coverage floor on real measured coverage.
Ratchet the coverage floor upward.
Require an explicit PR reason for lowering a coverage threshold.
Use production migrations and temporary SQLite files in integration tests.
Test migration from every supported prior schema.
Test transaction rollback and uniqueness constraints.
Test claim and budget concurrency.
Test restart and intent/receipt recovery.
Test subprocess timeout, signal forwarding, and process-tree termination.
Test workspace-path and symlink-escape rejection.
Run touched process/filesystem integration on Linux and macOS.
Keep a small cross-platform smoke suite on both hosted runner types.
Run deeper platform suites on `main` and a schedule when PR cost becomes material.
Cover WSL with a documented release smoke until trustworthy automation exists.
Do not describe Linux CI as Windows-host/WSL networking proof.
Build the production server and Vite assets from a frozen lockfile.
Start the built server, check health/readiness, and prove Fastify serves UI and Control API on one port.
Fail builds on missing environment declarations.
Fail builds on stale generated artifacts.
Fail when the browser bundle includes server-only modules or secret-bearing configuration.
Run Playwright against the built application, not only Vite preview/development.
Cover bootstrap/login.
Cover the operations dashboard.
Cover issue history.
Cover live-log reconnection.
Cover settings validation.
Cover stale-version rejection.
Cover authorization failure.
Cover safe rendering of hostile issue and log content.
Retain Playwright report, screenshots, traces, and relevant redacted server logs on failure.
Use synthetic browser-test credentials and data.
Build the production image for PRs without publishing it.
Run the image as its declared non-root user; check health/readiness and persistence; inspect user/entrypoint.
Scan the final image for vulnerabilities and accidentally included credentials.
Exclude `.git`, local databases, workspaces, environment files, coverage, browser artifacts, and unintended dependency directories from the build context.
Re-run the complete canonical graph on each resulting `main` commit.
Do not trust a PR result for a different synthetic merge SHA.
Block publication and release from a failed `main` commit and treat the failure as a repository incident.
Retain the built server and static UI bundle after successful `main` validation.
Retain a production OCI image after successful `main` validation.
Retain an SPDX or CycloneDX SBOM.
Retain vulnerability scan results.
Retain cryptographic provenance through GitHub attestations where available.
Retain checksums and immutable identifiers for every artifact.
Identify and promote the OCI image by digest.
Publish the image to GHCR under an immutable commit-SHA tag.
Make automation and deployments consume a digest or immutable tag.
Isolate package-write, attestation, and identity-token permissions in publication; keep test jobs read-only.
Publish artifacts built from the verified commit.
Do not rebuild source after approval or between environments.
End the pipeline at verified artifact publication until a deployment target exists.
Do not report an environment deployment before a target exists.
When environments exist, promote the same digest through staging and production.
When environments exist, record the environment and digest in GitHub.
When environments exist, run post-deployment health and functional checks.
Use semantic release tags of the form `vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`.
Point a release tag to a `main` commit whose full verification and artifact publication succeeded.
Verify the release tag and referenced commit.
Locate the existing immutable image and build artifacts for the commit.
Rerun security-policy checks whose data may have changed since build.
Attach checksums, SBOM, provenance, and human-readable release notes.
Add immutable semantic-version tags without replacing the commit-SHA tag.
Record failed promotion without deleting the previously published artifact.
Do not move stable release tags.
Give a broken release a new version or explicit yank and retain its evidence.
Configure a GitHub-integrated updater for npm/pnpm, GitHub Actions, and Docker.
Make the updater inspect the entire workspace and root lockfile.
Open security updates promptly.
Do not make a supported security fix wait for the ordinary batch.
Put major updates in separate pull requests.
Keep named compatibility-sensitive dependencies individually visible when their release-note surfaces differ.
Retain full-SHA execution pins when GitHub Actions update.
Resolve container-base updates to a reviewed immutable digest.
Make every dependency-update PR pass the normal gates.
Require human review for runtime, native, security-sensitive, migration, and major updates.
Produce the lockfile with the pinned pnpm version.
Do not repair or refresh dependency metadata during CI verification; use the frozen lockfile.
Disable install scripts by default or explicitly allowlist them when compatibility permits.
Review direct dependencies periodically and remove unused packages.
Make `make setup` install the pinned package manager/dependencies and repository-owned hooks.
Use Husky and lint-staged pinned through the root lockfile.
Declare hook behavior in version-controlled files.
Do not depend on a developer's global Git config for hook behavior.
Keep the pre-commit hook fast and operate on the staged snapshot.
Run Biome formatting and safe lint fixes on supported staged source.
Run Markdownlint CLI2 on staged documentation.
Reject conflict markers, trailing whitespace, and accidental large generated/binary files.
Run Gitleaks in staged/pre-commit mode without uploading content.
Restage only files selected by lint-staged.
Do not run complete unit, browser, integration, or container suites in pre-commit.
Do not rewrite unrelated unstaged files or stage unselected new files.
Validate the squash-title Conventional Commit subset with a commit-message hook.
Keep the PR workflow authoritative over the bypassable local commit hook.
Keep any materially slow pre-push hook optional.
Require `make verify` before requesting review and keep it the canonical local reproduction of CI.
Describe hook bypass as an exception, not the ordinary agent workflow.
Subject agent-authored commits to the same hooks and report every bypass in the PR.
Do not trust-cache `node_modules`, build output, mutable SQLite databases, credentials, or agent workspaces.
Make a cache miss produce the same result; treat cache hits only as performance hints, not evidence.
Do not restore executable artifacts from an untrusted fork cache in protected publication/deployment jobs.
Retain failed-test artifacts for at least 14 days.
Keep release artifacts, SBOMs, attestations, checksums, and deployment records under the project release-retention policy.
Do not let release artifacts, SBOMs, attestations, checksums, or deployment records disappear merely because an Actions run expires.
Treat issue text, logs, commands, and browser traces as potentially sensitive.
Redact and repository-access-control sensitive artifacts with the shortest diagnostic retention.
Treat canceled, timed-out, unexpectedly skipped, or evidence-incomplete required checks as unsuccessful.
Keep the original infrastructure failure visible when rerunning it.
Fix or explicitly revert a product-test failure; do not rerun it until green.
Record each flaky test as a defect with an owner and bounded repair date.
Quarantine only with an issue, narrow scope, and replacement required check covering the safety property.
Do not use silent retries, permanent quarantine, or blanket `continue-on-error` for required behavior.
Document rollback for every deployed environment before automatic deployment.
Roll back to a previously verified artifact digest.
Do not rebuild an old Git revision with new dependency resolution during rollback.
Require a compatible migration rollback or forward-repair plan before deployment.
Provide stable `make setup` for pinned dependencies and hooks.
Provide stable `make format`.
Provide stable `make lint` for formatting, workflow, and documentation policy.
Provide stable `make typecheck` for no-emit TypeScript.
Provide stable `make test` for the normal Vitest suite.
Provide stable `make test-integration`.
Provide stable `make test-e2e` against a production build.
Provide stable `make build` for production server/browser artifacts.
Provide stable `make image` for a local production image.
Provide stable `make verify-fast` for lint, typecheck, and unit tests.
Provide stable `make verify` as the complete non-publishing gate.
Make targets fail on the first invalid required result while preserving available reports.
Make CI wrappers call repository targets instead of maintaining a second correctness definition.
Land documentation, workflow lint, branch rules, and dependency update configuration with the code they validate.
Land pinned Node/pnpm, frozen install, Biome, and TypeScript checks as the second layer.
Land Vitest unit/contract suites and a coverage floor as the third layer.
Land SQLite migration and process integration suites as the fourth layer.
Land production build and Playwright tests as the fifth layer.
Land Docker runtime verification, scan, SBOM, and attestation as the sixth layer.
Land immutable `main` artifact publication as the seventh layer.
Add environment promotion only after a real target and rollback contract exist.
Mark every temporarily absent check as not implemented.
Do not use documentation or an empty passing job as proof of an unimplemented safety property.
